title: Creamy Ethiopian Tomato Stir-fry
description: A sour and creamy brunch-mood ethiopian dish featuring chimichurri sauce, cooked using a pressure cooker for an easy and fast meal.
instructions: Preheat your pressure cooker according to the manufacturer's instructions.
In a large pan over medium heat, sauté finely chopped onions and garlic until translucent.
Add chopped tomatoes along with a pinch of salt, pepper, and optional chili peppers. Cook for about 5 minutes until slightly softened.
Pour in chicken broth or water and bring to a simmer. Reduce heat to low and let it cook for another 10-15 minutes while the pressure cooker is heating up.
Meanwhile, mix together cream cheese and tomato paste in a bowl. If using a blender, blend this mixture until smooth.
Once the tomatoes are cooked through, add the blended cream cheese mixture into the pan with the tomatoes, stir well to combine all ingredients evenly.

If you're not using a pressure cooker, reduce heat further to maintain a gentle simmer for another 10 minutes. If your pressure cooker is hot enough, increase the temperature and cook under high pressure for about 5-7 minutes (consult your manual).
Remove from heat once done and transfer to a serving dish.
Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves before serving.
